title: marsdenia suborbicularis
intro: Marsdenia suborbicularis, also known as the Indian root or the milk vine, is a plant species native to the tropical regions of Asia and the Pacific Islands. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating fever, cough, and respiratory issues, due to its anti-inflammatory and expectorant properties. The plant's health benefits include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs, and acting as an antispasmodic, making it a popular herbal remedy for various ailments. Marsdenia suborbicularis is often used in herbal preparations such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are used to treat a range of health conditions, including bronchitis and asthma.
